description: Fix misleading error message in fetchJSONImpl when a non-204 response has content-length: 0. The current code treats any response with content-length: 0 identically to a 204, including using '204 No Content' in the error message even when the actual status is different (e.g., 200). This produces a confusing error message when schema validation is required and the response has no body.

userStories.title: Fix fetchJSONImpl error message for non-204 empty-body responses
userStories.description: As a plugin developer, I want fetchJSONImpl to report the actual HTTP status code in the error message when a response has content-length: 0, so that I can accurately diagnose why schema validation failed.
acceptanceCriteria: When fetchJSONImpl receives a 204 response with a schema, the error message includes '204 No Content'
When fetchJSONImpl receives a non-204 response (e.g., 200) with content-length: 0 and a schema, the error message includes the actual status code (e.g., 'HTTP 200') instead of '204 No Content'
When fetchJSONImpl receives any response with content-length: 0 and no schema, it still returns undefined (existing behavior preserved)
A test covers the case where a 200 response has content-length: 0 and a schema is provided, verifying the error message contains the actual status
A test covers the case where a 200 response has content-length: 0 and no schema is provided, verifying undefined is returned
